name: Check Into Cash
description_short: Check Into Cash is located at 2715 S 108th St, West Allis, WI 53227. Google reviewers rate the branch 4.9 stars across 452 reviews. Call +1 414-604-9051.
description_long:
Check Into Cash was founded over 30 years ago and has grown into one of the largest payday lending chains in the United States, operating retail storefronts across numerous states including Michigan. The company markets itself as a provider of short-term financial solutions for consumers facing unexpected expenses between paychecks, positioning its services under the tagline 'avoid a money mayday.' The Ferndale, Michigan location at 749 East 9 Mile Road is representative of the company's brick-and-mortar model, offering both in-store and online application options.

The core product at the Ferndale location is the Michigan Payday Loan, available up to $600, with funds disbursed on the same visit for in-store applicants. The national platform also offers Installment Loans for larger borrowing needs, Title Loans using a vehicle's clean title as collateral, and a Flex Line of Credit that allows revolving borrowing up to an approved credit limit. Beyond lending, the Ferndale store provides a full suite of ancillary financial services: check cashing, bill pay, Western Union money transfers, Green Dot Visa Debit Cards, and money orders. Tax preparation services are also available at select locations per the company's national site.

What distinguishes Check Into Cash from competitors is its longevity and multi-service retail model. The ability to cash a check, send a wire transfer, and apply for a loan in a single visit appeals to underbanked consumers who rely on in-person financial services. Hours at the Ferndale location run until 7pm on weekdays, with Saturday availability, reducing barriers for working customers. Applications require a government-issued ID, an active checking account with routing number, a Social Security number, and proof of income — a relatively accessible threshold compared to traditional lenders.

Honestly, Check Into Cash operates in a segment of consumer finance characterized by very high APRs. Michigan payday loans are subject to state fee schedules (linked from the Ferndale page), and short repayment cycles — typically tied to the borrower's next paydate — create rollover risk for consumers who cannot repay in full. The $600 Michigan payday loan cap limits the product's utility for larger emergencies. Consumers with access to credit unions, employer advances, or CDFI products will almost always find lower-cost alternatives. Check Into Cash is best understood as a high-cost convenience lender of last resort, not a wealth-building or credit-building tool.
pros: 30 years in operation, indicating institutional stability and regulatory compliance experience
Same-visit cash disbursement available for in-store applicants if approved
Multiple loan types offered nationally: payday, installment, title, and flex line of credit
Ancillary services (check cashing, Western Union, bill pay, Green Dot cards) available in one location
Extended weekday hours until 7pm and Saturday availability at the Ferndale location
Both in-store and online application options for payday loans
State license and retail fee schedule publicly linked on the store page for transparency
cons: Payday loans carry high fees typical of the industry; Michigan fee schedule applies and APRs are not disclosed in the provided content
Michigan payday loan capped at $600, which may be insufficient for larger unexpected expenses
Repayment is due on the next paydate, creating rollover risk if the borrower cannot repay in full
Requires an active checking account, excluding fully unbanked consumers from the loan product
Sunday hours unavailable at the Ferndale location
best_for: Employed consumers with an active checking account who need up to $600 before their next paycheck
Underbanked individuals who need check cashing, bill pay, or money transfer services alongside short-term lending
Borrowers who prefer in-person service and same-day cash over online-only lenders
Consumers who have exhausted lower-cost options and need a regulated, licensed lender as a last resort
diagnosis: Check Into Cash is best suited for employed, banked consumers who need a small amount of emergency cash quickly and have no lower-cost alternatives available. The main caveat is that payday loan fees translate to very high effective APRs, and the short repayment window tied to the next paydate creates meaningful risk of a debt cycle if the borrower's financial situation does not improve before repayment is due.
services: Payday Loans (up to $600 in Michigan, in-store and online)
Flex Line of Credit (revolving credit up to approved limit)
